document.title: Post Market Clinical Follow-up for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: EN ISO 13485[2016] Medical devices - Quality management systems - Requirements for regulatory purposes
MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 – Clinical Evaluation: Guide for manufacturers and notified bodies
MEDDEV 2.12/1 [Jan. 2013 Rev.8] Guidelines on medical device vigilance system
MEDDEV 2.12/2 [Jan. 2012] Guidelines on Post Market Clinical Follow up studies

sourceSummary.oneLine: PMCF document A-PMCF-01 Rev.0 for H&abyz digital flat panel X-ray detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports post-market clinical follow-up planning and explains how PMCF data are used for risk management and post-market surveillance.
keyPoints: The PMCF objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of the device, assess acceptability of identified risks, and detect emerging risks.
The device is a wired digital X-ray flat panel detector for general radiographic systems and is not to be used for mammography.
The document concludes that a PMCF study is not required because safety and clinical performance are already known from previous use and post-market surveillance can provide sufficient data.

evidence.claim: The document number is A-PMCF-01 and the revision is 0.
evidence.evidenceText: Document No. A-PMCF-01 Establish date. Sep/12/2019 Revision No. 0

evidence.claim: The document was established on Sep/12/2019.
The product models covered are HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
The device class is Class IIa.
H&abyz Co., Ltd. is the manufacturer.
The detector is intended for general radiographic systems for human anatomy and to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy. It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The detector is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The PMCF objective is to confirm clinical performance and safety, assess acceptability of identified risks, and detect emerging risks.
evidence.evidenceText: The objective of the Post Market Clinical Follow-Up (PMCF) is to confirm clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of the medical device, the acceptability of identified risks and to detect emerging risks on the basis of factual evidence.

evidence.claim: The document states that a PMCF study is not required for the digital X-ray flat panel detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The PMCF study is not required for digital x-ray flat panel detector, because the safety and clinical performance are already known from previous use of the device in 20 years.

evidence.claim: If additional PMCF research is needed according to circumstance checks, the PMCF plan will proceed.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the above PMCF plan, if additional PMCF research is needed according to circumstance check, we will proceed the PMCF plan.
